scenario: Incomplete AE — a pharmacist reports a walk-in customer's reaction, but the PATIENT is not identifiable (no name/age/details). Reporter/product/event present, patient pillar missing -> is_valid_icsr = False. Routes to PV (Gate 4); the 4-pillar panel shows DRUG/EVENT/REPORTER valid but PATIENT VERIFY -> outreach needed. Fills the 'not submission-ready' coverage gap.
input_text:
SOURCE CHANNEL: Email Inbound Gateway
PRIMARY DISPATCH LOG: safety-intake@cardiomedglobal.com

MESSAGE BODY:
Hi CardioMed,

I'm a community pharmacist. This morning a customer came to my counter quite distressed — they said they had developed significant facial and lip swelling a few hours after taking CholoClear-X, which they'd just picked up. Before I could take down their name or any details, they rushed off to an urgent care clinic. I don't have their information, but I felt I should report the reaction in case it's important for your safety monitoring.

Regards,
Gregory Pratt, RPh
Cornerstone Community Pharmacy
Email: gpratt@cornerstonerx.com

failed_gates: Gate 4: Adverse-event policy — AE flag set -> PV (never auto-approve)
Gate 6: HCP link — reporter not found in HCP registry

labeling_notes: INCOMPLETE ICSR (the point of this case): reporter (the pharmacist), product (CholoClear-X), and event (facial/lip swelling = possible angioedema) are present, but there is NO identifiable patient -> has_patient_pillar False -> is_valid_icsr False. The 4-pillar panel shows PATIENT as VERIFY, not submission-ready -> PV must conduct outreach for the missing patient identifier. All other PV golden cases are submission-ready; this exercises the outreach state.

SERIOUS: facial + lip swelling reads as possible angioedema (an Important Medical Event) and the customer left for urgent care -> labeled Serious. Over-calling seriousness errs in the safe direction for PV triage.
ROUTING unchanged: Gate 4 routes every AE to PV regardless of completeness. Gate 6 fails (the pharmacist is not in this tenant's HCP registry) — noted, not a blocker for an AE.
PRIVACY: no patient identifiers are invented — patient_initials/age/gender are all null because the source genuinely contains none.
